mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive directional bias when INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 2.5% force expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or Rank IC stays below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion and higher corporate profit expectations, driving immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or the signal fails to generate positive alpha during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization indicates supply bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or loses predictive significance when capacity utilization falls below 78%.
